thread.title: Mexican immigrant gets 40-year prison sentence for drug trafficking
thread.title_full: Mexican immigrant gets 40-year prison sentence for drug trafficking

author: Zacharia Washington
title: Mexican immigrant gets 40-year prison sentence for drug trafficking
text: A Mexican drug trafficker with links to a cartel was sentenced to 40 years in federal prison on Wednesday. Adriana Melendez-Calvillo, 43, pleaded guilty to a “criminal information charging conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute methamphetamine and money laundering,” last year in November, a news release from the U.S. Department of Justice said. She is a citizen of Mexico illegally present in the U.S., according to prosecutors. During Melendez-Calvillo’s sentencing on Wednesday, a federal agent testified that she had ties to Jalisco New Generation Cartel, or CJNG, which is a transnational drug trafficking organization. Court evidence introduced text messages that suggested Melendez-Calvillo received shipments of meth, cocaine and other drugs directly from a supplier in Mexico and she electronically transferred $828,094 in proceeds to individuals in the country, prosecutors said. Court evidence also uncovered that Melendez-Calvillo had parts in trafficking “at least 1,000 pounds of cocaine, 41 kilograms of methamphetamine and 7.8 kilograms of ice methamphetamine,” according to the release. One of Melendez-Calvillo’s co-conspirators, David Garcia, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute methamphetamine last November, the release said. His sentencing is scheduled for January. Another co-conspirator, Omar Gilliam, was charged with conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute methamphetamine and is currently awaiting trial. Garcia and Gilliam both played parts in a meth transaction with Melendez-Calvillo, according to prosecutors.
